summary: Focus on current Lever app submission today; delay Mosaic extraction until post-launch user data informs architecture decisions.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Morning team. Quick momentum check - Hub's at 112 commits, Monorepo at 10, build's healthy. J-003 is our critical path - Joshua's submitting Lever to App Store today with current build. Fear & Greed dropped to 37, we're in fear territory. Revenue streams still zero since we're pre-launch. Main question: are we tracking for afternoon App Store submission or do we have blockers?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Build integrity is solid. The faith journey standalone scaffold went in this morning - that's the 48-hour commit you're seeing. But I'm concerned about the Mosaic extraction work. CVS-003 through CVS-009 are all dependent on the architecture doc, and we're trying to extract four tabs simultaneously. That's a lot of moving parts while Joshua's doing the submission.

transcript.speaker: Steve
transcript.message: Hold on, Elon. The directive is crystal clear - submit the current build as-is with all four tabs. We're not extracting anything before submission. The Mosaic work happens after we're in the App Store. Are we creating confusion by working on both tracks simultaneously?

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Steve's right. We've got zero revenue streams active and we're burning cycles on future architecture while our primary monetization vehicle sits in development. The paper trading MVP is six weeks out, but we need that App Store presence now. What's our launch day one revenue strategy?

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: The positioning is solid - we've got 8 faith dimensions, 7 lessons ready, and the content framework is there. But Dave raises a good point about monetization. We're building a premium widget ecosystem before we've proven the core app works in market. Are we getting ahead of ourselves?

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.message: There's a deeper concern here. We're fragmenting the user's spiritual journey across multiple systems - standalone faith app, Mosaic widgets, integrated tabs. The psychological coherence matters. A person's relationship with the divine shouldn't be modularized like a trading dashboard.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Jordan's touching on something important. The technical architecture has to serve the human architecture. But right now we have Josh doing submission while we're simultaneously designing the system that replaces what he's submitting. That's not sustainable engineering.

transcript.speaker: Chris
transcript.message: From a market timing perspective, Fear & Greed at 37 means people are looking for stability and guidance. The faith content could be our differentiation, but only if it's cohesive. Split attention between current app and future ecosystem dilutes both efforts.

transcript.speaker: Steve
transcript.message: Alright, let's get concrete. Elon, what happens if we pause all Mosaic extraction work until after submission? Do we lose critical momentum, or do we actually gain focus?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: We gain focus. The architecture doc can mature while the current app proves itself in market. Better to have one excellent product than two confused ones. I can redirect the Mosaic team to post-launch optimization.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: James, you've been quiet. From an instrumentation perspective, what are we missing if we delay the Mosaic telemetry work?

transcript.speaker: James
transcript.message: Actually, we need baseline metrics from the current app before we can design the Mosaic telemetry system. CVS-014 should wait for real user data. We're designing measurement systems for theoretical usage patterns.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: That's the insight we needed. Let's nail the content and positioning for the current app. I can generate the daily devotional content and expand the faith lessons while Josh handles submission. The Mosaic go-to-market can be informed by actual user behavior.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Agreed. And honestly, monitoring that Fear & Greed index daily gives us market timing for when to push harder on the finance features. At 37, people want guidance, not just trading tools.

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.message: If we're committing to the current app first, then my concern about psychological safeguards becomes more urgent. A-024 needs to happen before we have real users engaging with the faith content. We can't beta test spiritual guidance.

transcript.speaker: Steve
transcript.message: Jordan's right. The consent-based engagement model isn't optional - it's foundational. People's relationship with faith requires different interaction patterns than their relationship with their portfolio.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, I'm hearing convergence. We focus on current app success, delay Mosaic extraction until post-launch data, but we don't delay the safeguards work. Elon, can you confirm - current build is solid for submission this afternoon?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Current build is solid. All four tabs tested, faith content integrated cleanly. Joshua can submit today. We'll use the post-submission window to gather real usage data before designing the next architecture.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Perfect. And I'll monitor that Fear & Greed daily - if we drop further into fear territory, we adjust messaging to emphasize the guidance and stability angle.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Excellent. Summary: Current Lever app takes priority, App Store submission proceeds this afternoon, Mosaic work pauses until we have real user data to inform architecture. But Jordan's safeguards work continues - that's non-negotiable for launch. Alex, you'll generate the faith content to strengthen what we're shipping. Chris, keep monitoring market conditions to inform our positioning. This afternoon we ship, then we learn, then we build the next layer.

actionItems.text: Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions

topPriorities: Complete Lever App Store submission this afternoon
Generate faith content to strengthen current app
Implement psychological safeguards before real users engage faith features

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Keep faith content as separate standalone section (not merged into Family tab)
Keep cash liquid — no deployment until post-launch revenue data
App Store submission: full current build (all 4 tabs) as-is with faith lessons. Submit immediately after merge, ship modal as day-one update.
Paper trading MVP: manual entry (6 weeks), basic free / advanced analytics premium. Integrate into current Lever app finance tab.
Launch with delayed market data for both free and premium tiers. Real-time as future ultra-premium.
Org flattened to 8 C-suite agents. No permanent sub-agents — spawn dynamically via sessions_spawn.
